items.title: Ford bets on Apple for its next-generation of EVs
items.content: Ford is the first automaker to use a new set of developer tools that will let it embed Apple Maps navigation and mapping directly into its vehicles' infotainment systems.

items.title: Apple teams up with Klarna to launch a lease-to-own program for iPhones, iPads, and Macs
items.content: The new leasing program is a big change for the hardware company, and comes as it looks to raise prices on many of its products.

items.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
items.content: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast a shadow over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.

items.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
items.content: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy.

items.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
items.content: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

items.title: Microsoft patches bug in video game Age of Empires II
items.content: The vulnerability in the decades-old game could have allowed hackers to take over victims’ computers with a malicious game invite.

items.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
items.content: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI lab

items.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
items.content: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

items.title: China Rewrites the ‘Soft Power’ Playbook for the A.I. Age
items.content: China is seeking global influence and good will with its open, low-cost artificial intelligence software.

items.title: Movie Theaters Embrace the Bewilderingly Lucrative Business of Buckets
items.content: Once, a tub of popcorn was just a tub of popcorn. Now, “collectible concession vessels” could make one chain more than $100 million.

items.title: Even as War Rages, Investors Bet Oil Will Be Cheaper in a Few Months
items.content: Futures contracts reflect an expectation that low inventories and midterm elections are likely to push the Trump administration to try to ease tensions with Iran.

items.title: Global Oil Price Hits $100 a Barrel for First Time Since May
items.content: The recent rebound reflects traders’ growing concern about the expanding war, which now extends from the Persian Gulf to the Red Sea.
